_comment: Curated sealed-product membership data, merged into `sealed_contents` ONLY for products
MTGJSON leaves with no contents. MTGJSON (AllPrintings.json) is the authoritative source;
this file fills genuine upstream gaps — products whose `contents` are un-authored so their
cards would otherwise show no sealed products at all. See api/src/mtgjson/fallback.rs and the
A fallback product is applied per-product only when MTGJSON emitted ZERO rows for it, so a
product MTGJSON later starts describing silently supersedes this file (no code change).
Cards are keyed by (set, number) matching cards.set_code/collector_number; `name` is
documentation only. `membership` is contains | booster | variable (the 'found in / can be
pulled from / may be in' buckets). `foil` marks a foil-only inclusion.
Editing this file changes its content hash, which the ingest folds into its version gate so
the merge re-runs on the next sync even if MTGJSON itself is unchanged.

products.name: Avatar: The Last Airbender - Commander's Bundle
note: MTGJSON ships contents:null for this bundle (mtg-sealed-content TLA.yaml has no contents
block authored), so its borderless Commander-staple reprints (Avatar 'Eternal' set tle,
collector numbers 305-317) resolve to no sealed product. The bundle guarantees three
staples (Arcane Signet, Sol Ring, Swiftfoot Boots) and includes two more drawn from a pool
of ten — so the guaranteed three are `contains` and the pool is `variable`. All are the
nonfoil borderless printing. Source: Wizards 'Collecting Avatar' article + retail listings.
